hero.title2: built and set up for you.
hero.body: We build your digital menu, style it to your brand, link it into your existing website, and train your team. You change a dish in two taps.
hero.avatars:
Loved by independent venues
across the country

feat.title: Built around your menu and your brand, not a one-size-fits-all template.
feat.c1_body: Photos, descriptions, allergens, prices — all laid out so diners actually read them.
feat.c2_title: A dashboard you can run from the bar
feat.c2_body: Edit a dish, swap a photo, mark something sold out — from any phone or laptop, in seconds.
feat.c3_body: Dish popularity, views, and trends — so your next menu is based on data, not guesswork.

how.title: We set it up. You run it.
how.s1_body: PDF, a phone photo of the chalkboard, a Word doc — whatever you've got. We take it from there.
how.s2_body: Matched to your brand, your tone, your venue. Table ordering, coupon codes, custom sections — whatever your restaurant needs, we build it in.
how.s3_body: Plugged into your existing website. A 30-minute training call with your team. That's the handover.
how.s4_body: Edit a dish in 10 seconds. See what's selling. We're still there when you need us.
how.quote: "Most people go live in 5–7 days from sending us their menu. The training call is the longest meeting we ask of you."

diff.body: Most digital menu products give you a builder, a help centre, and a free trial. We don't — we build it, set it up, and walk your team through it.
diff.p1: We build your menu from whatever you have — PDF, photo, doc.
diff.p2: We style it to match your brand and venue.
diff.p3: We link it into your existing website ourselves.
diff.p4: We train your team in a single 30-minute session.
diff.card_body: From signed contract to live menu in days, not weeks.

proof.quote: Placeholder testimonial — the kind of quote that says "we don't have to think about the menu anymore." Real client quote goes here.

price.title: A one-time setup fee, then a flat monthly subscription.
price.body: Hosting, support, and updates included. No per-cover charges, no per-dish charges, no surprise invoices.

cta.title: Let's get your menu live.
cta.body: A 20-minute demo. We'll show you the dashboard, walk through real venue setups, and answer your questions. No commitment.

features.title: Everything you need to run your menu. Nothing you don't.
features.intro: Four parts to Dimonova: the digital menu your diners see, the dashboard your team runs it from, the analytics that tell you what's working, and the done-for-you onboarding that gets all of it live.

f1.title: A menu that looks like your venue, not a takeaway app.
f1.body: Photos, descriptions, allergens, prices, sections, daily specials — properly typeset. Loads fast on a phone. Lives inside your existing website, so diners never feel handed off to a third-party tool.
f1.p1: Embedded, not redirected. Lives at yourvenue.com/menu, not a generic URL.
f1.p2: Allergens & dietary tags on every dish, filterable for diners.
f1.p3: Optional QR codes — table cards, posters, business cards — point straight to it.
f1.p4: Sold-out states flip a dish off instantly during service.

f2.title: If you can use Instagram, you can use this.
f2.body: No menu engineer training, no agency tickets, no "raise a support request to change a price." Whoever's running the floor can flip a dish off, raise a price, or swap a photo from their phone in under a minute.
f2.p1: Changes go live instantly — no publishing queue, no waiting on a developer.
f2.p2: Menu in multiple languages — guests see it in their language automatically.
f2.p3: Fully personalised to your brand — colours, fonts, and feel built for your restaurant.
f2.p4: Works on any device. Bartender's phone, manager's iPad, owner's laptop.

f3.title: The menu is finally giving you data, not guesses.
f3.body: Every dish view is logged. You see what diners look at, what they skip, and what's losing them at the description. Run a brunch special; see if anyone read it. Your next menu refresh is decisions, not opinions.
f3.p1: Dish popularity by week and month — see what's selling and when.
f3.p2: Active dish overview — all your live items at a glance.
f3.p3: Custom categories — organise your menu sections your way.

f4.title: The part nobody else does. We do it for you.
f4.c1_body: Send us your menu in whatever format you have. We type it up, structure it, organise it into sections, add allergens, and check every dish with you before going live.
f4.c2_body: Colours, fonts, photography style — matched to your venue. The menu should feel like it belongs to your brand, not ours. We'll take cues from your existing site and signage.
f4.c3_body: Embedded directly into your existing website by us. Squarespace, Wix, WordPress, custom build — we handle it. Your menu lives at yourvenue.com/menu, not on someone else's domain.
f4.c4_body: A single 30-minute session with whoever will run the dashboard — usually the owner and the floor manager. We screen-share, you take notes. After that, you're independent.

cta.title: See it on your menu, not ours.
cta.body: Book a demo and we'll mock up a section of your real menu — no commitment, no slides.

pricing.title: Priced around your venue.
pricing.intro: Tell us about your venue and we'll build a number around it. Here's what that always covers.

included.title: Everything every client gets, regardless of venue size.
included.i1: Menu structured and built by us, from whatever you have
included.i2: Styled to match your existing brand
included.i3: Embedded into your existing website — no new site needed
included.i4: Unlimited dish, price, and section updates — done by you, instantly
included.i5: Dish popularity analytics and weekly digest
included.i6: Hosting, security, and software updates — always on
included.i7: Real human support — email and WhatsApp
included.i8: QR codes for tables and print (optional add-on)

shape.intro: A few things inform the number — and we talk through all of them on the call.
shape.i2: Photography — your own, sourced, or arranged
shape.i3: One menu or several (drinks, seasonal, events)
shape.i5: How your current website is built

strip.title: Ready to talk numbers?
strip.body: Tell us about your venue and we'll put a quote together on the call.

faq.title: Common questions
faq.a1: A simple service agreement, cancellable on a month's notice. No multi-year lock-ins.
faq.q2: What if my menu changes a lot?
faq.a2: Edit it yourself, as often as you like. Your monthly rate doesn't change based on how often you update things.
faq.q3: Do I need a new website?
faq.a3: No. Your existing one stays. We embed the menu into it. Squarespace, Wix, WordPress, or a custom build — all fine.
faq.q4: What about photos of the dishes?
faq.a4: You can supply your own, use ours from your existing site, or skip photos entirely. Optional add-on: we arrange a photographer.
faq.a5: Five to seven working days from receiving your menu, in most cases. We'll commit to a date on the demo call.

cta.title: Want to be the next case study?

about.title: A small team. A specific job.
about.intro: Dimonova exists because the menu — the most-read page of any restaurant's website — was being treated as an afterthought. We build it, run it, and look after it for you.

why.title: Most digital menus are bad. The good ones are too much work.
why.p1: Every restaurant has the same problem: the menu changes constantly, but the website never reflects it. PDFs are out of date. The website agency is on a two-week ticket queue. The QR code product is a nightmare to edit. Somewhere along the way, the menu — the thing diners actually want to look at — became the worst-maintained part of the venue's online presence.
why.p2: We built Dimonova because that problem isn't really a software problem. It's a service problem. Software doesn't make your menu professional — a designer does. Software doesn't link it to your website — a developer does. Software doesn't train your team — a person does. So we do all of that, and the software is what makes it cheap enough to make sense.
why.p3: We work with restaurants, pubs, and cafés. We don't work with chains — bespoke setup wouldn't scale, and they don't need it.
why.aside_quote: "We make digital menus for places that take their food seriously and don't want to think about software."

principles.title: Three principles, and not many more.
principles.p1_body: If onboarding takes the customer more than 30 minutes of their time, we've designed it wrong.
principles.p2_body: Flat monthly. No per-cover charges, no per-dish charges, no surprise invoices on busy weeks.
principles.p3_body: The menu lives on the venue's domain, in the venue's style. We're the ones quietly making it work.
